result.summary: sourceforge.io exposes three documented HTTP APIs on SourceForge-hosted endpoints: the Allura REST API, a public Download Stats API, and a File Release API; no GraphQL, MCP server, or SourceForge-issued CLI was found.
result.description: SourceForge is a software directory and project-hosting platform for open-source projects, downloads, reviews, and related collaboration tools. It also provides hosted project services such as repositories, tickets, wikis, discussions, and file releases.

sf_oauth_app.label: SourceForge OAuth consumer / bearer token
sf_oauth_app.setup: Sign in to your SourceForge account, open the [OAuth account page](https://sourceforge.net/auth/oauth/), and create an OAuth consumer for your application. From the same page you can view the consumer key/secret and generate a bearer token. Use the bearer token for your own scripts, or use the consumer key/secret to run the documented OAuth 1.0 authorization flow for third-party apps.

sf_release_api_key.label: SourceForge Releases API key
sf_release_api_key.setup: Sign in to your SourceForge account, open your account page, and click `Generate` under `Releases API Key` as described in [Using the Release API](https://sourceforge.net/p/forge/documentation/Using%20the%20Release%20API/). Copy the generated key into your release script or local config.

variables.name: project
variables.description: SourceForge project slug, e.g. `openofficeorg.mirror` from the documented example URL.

requiredHeaders.name: Accept
requiredHeaders.description: Optional but documented to ensure a JSON response.

variables.name: file_path
variables.description: Path to the file within the project's Files area; documented by removing `/download` from the file URL.
